title: Module 1
content:
Introduction, First Law of Thermodynamics - Energy Balance for closed
and open Systems- application of first law to analyse flow through
horizontal pipe and nozzle.  Limitations of First Law. Second Law of
Thermodynamics - definition of entropy – calculation of entropy changes
in processes involving ideal gases, Third law of Thermodynamics.
Fundamental property relations, Maxwell’s Equations, Clausius-
Clapeyron equation, Entropy-heat capacity relationships - equations for
entropy, internal energy and enthalpy in terms of measurable quantities,
Joule-Thomson coefficient, Gibbs Helmholtz Equation.
Problem solving in application of laws of thermodynamics, and property
calculations  using EXCEL / Matlab / SCILAB / PYTHON etc. – only for
selfstudy / microproject  /assignment

title: Module 2
content:
P-V and P-T diagram of pure substances, Cubic Equations of state for
real gases (Concept of Redlich Kwong, Peng Robinson and Virial
equations)Principle
of
corresponding
states
and
generalized
compressibility chart, Concept of Residual properties, Estimation of
Fugacity and activity of pure fluids - effect of temperature and pressure
on fugacity and activity.
Definition of partial molar properties and chemical potential, Fugacity in
solution, Ideal solution, Lewis-Randall rule - Raoult’s law, Henry’s law,
Activity in solutions, Activity coefficient - Gibbs-Duhem equations,
property changes of mixing, excess properties.
Problem solving in Equations of state, properties of ideal solutions using
EXCEL/Matlab/SCILAB/PYTHON
etc.
–
only
for
selfstudy/microproject/assignment

title: Module 3
content:
Criterion of phase equilibrium – equations for single component and
multicomponent equilibria, Gibbs Phase rule, Duhem’s theorem.
Vapour-liquid equilibrium- phase diagram for binary solutions- VLE in
ideal solutions, non-ideal solutions, azeotropes, equations for VLE at low
pressures and VLE at high pressures Activity coefficient models (General
concepts of Margules equation, Wohl’s equation -Wilson equation - NRTL,
UNIQUAC and UNIFAC models), calculations based on Van Laar
equation
Vapour Liquid-Liquid Equilibrium in partially miscible and immiscible
systems.
Calculations
in
Vapour
liquid
Equilibrium
using
EXCEL/Matlab/SCILAB/PYTHON
etc.
–only
for
selfstudy/microproject/assignment

title: Module 4
content:
Chemical reaction equilibria: Extent of reaction, equilibrium constant,
standard free energy change, feasibility of reaction, effect of temperature
on equilibrium constant, evaluation of equilibrium constant, Equilibrium
conversion in gas-phase reactions, Effect of pressure and other
parameters on conversion, Simultaneous reactions, liquid phase reactions,
pressure of decomposition, Phase-rule for reacting systems.
Problem
solving
in
Reaction
equilibrium
using
EXCEL/Matlab/SCILAB/PYTHON
etc.
–
only
for
selfstudy/microproject/assignment
